rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope and properly declared as helpers or features.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the actual types of expressions, especially for comparisons that require bool, int, or real types.
Avoid using implementation-specific identifiers or subcomponent ports in component type contracts; only use visible features or declared helpers.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure that named things are valid expressions with well-defined types to prevent '<error>' type diagnostics.
Use only visible AADL features or helpers declared within the same annex when writing guarantees or assumptions.
When writing guarantees, relate outputs to inputs using visible identifiers; avoid implementation-only paths in type-level contracts.
Match scalar type names in AGREE declarations to valid AADL types: int, real, bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE.
Ensure that all constants and equations in AGREE annexes are declared with correct scalar types (int, real, bool).
Place assumptions and guarantees in appropriate scopes: component type contracts for input/output relations, implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current AGREE annex; this leads to '<error>' type diagnostics.
Do not mix package-qualified names in AGREE unless they are explicitly imported via 'with' clauses in the AADL model.
Avoid placing implementation-specific subcomponent ports in component type annexes; use only visible features or helpers.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
